title: Wallace Line Overview
text: The Wallace Line is a major biogeographical boundary running between Bali and Lombok and between Borneo and Sulawesi. Proposed by Alfred Russel Wallace, it separates Asian fauna from Australasian fauna. The boundary exists because deep ocean trenches prevented land crossing even during Ice Ages. The Weber Line lies east of it and represents the equilibrium zone between Asian and Australasian species. The Lydekker Line lies further east and marks the edge of the Australasian continental shelf. These lines explain Indonesia's unique biodiversity and evolutionary history.
